issues: Draft fails to mention Dave Tomlinson (ex-Sockeyes player, North Vancouver BC), who is the primary reason this clipping is in the Sockeyes archive. Tomlinson recorded three assists in the game, assisting on Kelfer's goal (period 2), Dion's goal (period 3), and Sacco's goal (period 3).

Draft says 'BU scored twice in the second period' — the OCR shows BU scored once in the second (Kelfer at 9:20) and BC scored twice (Shea and Scheifele). BU scored three in the first and three in the third.

The headline 'BU Beats BC 6-3 in Hockey East Game' is plausible but not confirmed from the OCR — the actual headline text is not visible in the OCR excerpt; the draft headline should be treated as uncertain.
No byline is visible in the OCR, which is correctly left blank, but the description focus is entirely wrong (no mention of Tomlinson).
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Boston University's Dave Tomlinson, a former Richmond Sockeye, recorded three assists as the Terriers defeated BC 6-3 in a Hockey East game at Walter Brown Arena on February 28, 1988.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Former Richmond Sockeyes player Dave Tomlinson contributed three assists for Boston University in a 6-3 Hockey East victory over BC at Walter Brown Arena on February 28, 1988. Tomlinson assisted on goals by Mike Kelfer (2nd period), Tom Dion (3rd period), and Joe Sacco (3rd period). Joe Sacco led BU with two goals and an assist, while BC's David Littman made 38 saves in a losing effort and BU's Peter Fish stopped 29 shots. The box score and game recap appear on page 70 of The Boston Globe.
review.notes: This clipping is archive-relevant because of Dave Tomlinson (ex-Sockeyes, North Vancouver BC), who had a standout 3-assist game for BU. The actual article headline is not legible in the OCR excerpt — editor should confirm from the scan image. The draft's scoring-period summary was also inaccurate per the OCR box score.
